text.headline: Ashley M. Matheny - Academic Chronicle
text.text: A timeline of education, research, field campaigns, awards, and scientific milestones in ecohydrology.

text.headline: Born in West Virginia
text.text: Born in a small town along the Ohio River, where water skiing, canoeing, and camping fostered a deep passion for nature.

text.headline: Ohio State University - BS Civil Engineering
text.text: Earned Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering from The Ohio State University, beginning research on tree water use in Michigan forests.

text.headline: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
text.text: Worked studying water interactions with dams for the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers after completing bachelor's degree.

text.headline: University Fellowship - Ohio State
text.text: Awarded University Fellowship from Ohio State University.

text.headline: PhD - Ohio State University
text.text: Doctorate in Civil, Environmental, and Geodetic Engineering from Ohio State University under advisor Gil Bohrer.

text.headline: Outstanding Student Paper Award - AGU
text.text: Received Outstanding Student Paper Award from American Geophysical Union Fall Meeting.

text.headline: Presidential Fellowship - Ohio State
text.text: Awarded Presidential Fellowship from Ohio State University.

text.headline: Postdoctoral Researcher - Ohio State
text.text: Postdoctoral Research Scientist in Civil, Environmental, and Geodetic Engineering at Ohio State University.

text.headline: Joined UT Austin Faculty
text.text: Appointed Assistant Professor in the Department of Geological Sciences at the Jackson School of Geosciences, University of Texas at Austin.

text.headline: UT Rising STARs Faculty Award
text.text: Received University of Texas Rising STARs Faculty Award.

text.headline: Michigan Field Site Established
text.text: Continued long-term sap flux and tree water content monitoring at University of Michigan Biological Station.

text.headline: New Mexico Field Campaign
text.text: Led field campaign in Valles Caldera, New Mexico studying groundwater-surface water interactions.

text.headline: Distinguished Teaching Award
text.text: Received G. Moses and Carolyn G. Knebel Distinguished Teaching Award from Jackson School of Geosciences.

text.headline: DOE Mangrove Research Project
text.text: Awarded U.S. Department of Energy grant to study mangrove ecosystem responses to salinity and climate change.

text.headline: NSF CAREER Award
text.text: Received prestigious NSF Faculty Early Career Development (CAREER) Award for research on biomass hydraulic capacitance.

text.headline: AMS Outstanding Early Career Award
text.text: Received American Meteorological Society's Outstanding Early Career Award in Agricultural and Forest Meteorology.

text.headline: Texas Global Seed Grant - Jamaica
text.text: Awarded Texas Global Seed Grant with Dr. Rowan Martindale to study mangrove-coral reef interactions in Jamaica.

text.headline: Continued Research Leadership
text.text: Leading active research program with multiple graduate students and postdocs studying ecohydrology across diverse ecosystems.
